title: Align operations supervisor with Phase 3 testnet semi-manual semantics
root_cause: testnet_operations_evidence_supervisor.py finalize() treated ALLOW_TESTNET_ORDER=true as a hard blocked_reason ('Testnet order gate is enabled'). That semantic predates CP-042: under Phase 2.97 the flag was only enabled briefly during a controlled roundtrip, so an enabled gate at idle was an anomaly. After the CP-042 Phase 3 unlock the flag is the approved semi-manual steady state, which made the supervisor return HALTED permanently and contradict the manual approval gate that requires ALLOW_TESTNET_ORDER=true. CP-044 preflight was therefore unpassable by design.
semantic_change_summary: ALLOW_TESTNET_ORDER=true alone no longer forces HALTED. It now sets phase3_armed=true and appends 'Testnet order gate is enabled (Phase 3 semi-manual armed state)' to a new soft_safety_warnings list that does not affect the verdict. The armed state is surfaced in the CLI summary ('Phase 3 Armed: YES' plus soft warning lines) and the Telegram preview. This mirrors the precedent set by commit 14f0a5e, which made the same flag a soft check in testnet_stability_policy.py.
hard_blockers_preserved: TESTNET_EXECUTION_HALT env or halt file active
non-demo or production Binance base URL
live non-flat position, open orders, or other non-zero positions
final flat state not verified (live check required for definitive verdict)
soft_warnings_added: Testnet order gate is enabled (Phase 3 semi-manual armed state)
full_suite_after: pytest tests/ (excluding test_manual_actual_testnet_roundtrip_controller.py, tracked separately): 506 passed, 0 failed, 24 subtests passed
